title: Workdown Schema
description: Validates the structure of a project's schema.yaml file, which defines work item fields and validation rules.

fields.description: Map of field names to field definitions. Each field picks a type from the type system and configures it.

type.description: The built-in type for this field.

required.description: Whether this field must be present on every work item.

default.description: Default value applied by `workdown add`. Can be a literal value or a generator ($filename, $filename_pretty, $uuid, $today, $max_plus_one).

values.description: Allowed values. Required for choice and multichoice types.

pattern.description: Regex pattern the value must match. Only valid for string type.

min.description: Minimum allowed value. Number for integer/float; suffix-shorthand duration string (e.g. "0s", "4w") for duration.

max.description: Maximum allowed value. Number for integer/float; suffix-shorthand duration string (e.g. "0s", "4w") for duration.

allow_cycles.description: Whether circular references are allowed. Only valid for link and links types.

inverse.description: Inverse relationship name for rules dot-notation traversal. Only valid for link and links types. Example: a 'parent' field with inverse 'children' enables 'children.type' references in rules.

resource.description: Name of a resource section in resources.yaml. The field value must match an id from that resource. Only valid for string and list types.

aggregate.description: Aggregation config for computed fields that propagate up the parent chain.
Aggregation config. The field is set manually on leaf items and computed automatically by walking the link field named in `over` (default 'parent') upward through the hierarchy.

function.description: The aggregation function. Available functions depend on the field type: integer/float/duration support sum, min, max, average, median, count; date supports min, max, average; boolean supports all, any, none, count.

error_on_missing.description: Whether to report an error if a leaf item in the hierarchy is missing this field.

over.description: Name of the link field to walk upward for the rollup. Must reference a `link` (single-valued) field in the schema. Defaults to 'parent' when omitted.

rules.description: Validation rules for cross-field, cross-item, and collection-wide constraints.

field_reference.description: A field name on the current item, or a dot-notation path that traverses a relationship. The first segment of a dot path must be a link/links field or its inverse (e.g. 'children' for the inverse of 'parent'). Only one level of traversal is supported.

condition.description: A condition that a field must satisfy. The YAML/JSON type determines the meaning: a scalar means equality, an array means membership (one of), an object uses an explicit operator.

oneOf.description: Equality: field must equal this string value.
Equality: field must equal this numeric value.
Equality: field must equal this boolean value.
Membership: field must be one of these values.
Explicit operator. When multiple operators are specified, all must be satisfied (AND).

not.description: Field must not equal this value. If an array, field must not equal any value in the list.

is_set.description: true = field must have a value; false = field must be null/absent.

all.description: Every related item must satisfy this condition. Only valid when the field reference traverses a one-to-many relationship (links field or inverse of a link field).

any.description: At least one related item must satisfy this condition. Only valid when the field reference traverses a one-to-many relationship.

none.description: No related item may satisfy this condition. Only valid when the field reference traverses a one-to-many relationship.

assertion.description: An assertion that must hold for a field. A keyword string ('required' or 'forbidden') for common checks, or an object with explicit operators.

oneOf.description: 'required' = field must be set; 'forbidden' = field must not be set.
Explicit operator. When multiple operators are specified, all must be satisfied (AND).

forbidden.description: true = field must NOT be set.

values.description: Field must be one of these values (subset of the field's defined values).

not.description: Field must not equal this value. If an array, field must not equal any value in the list.

eq_field.description: Field must equal the value of the referenced field.

lt_field.description: Field must be less than the value of the referenced field.

lte_field.description: Field must be less than or equal to the value of the referenced field.

gt_field.description: Field must be greater than the value of the referenced field.

gte_field.description: Field must be greater than or equal to the value of the referenced field.

min_count.description: Related items (via link/links) must number at least this many.

max_count.description: Related items (via link/links) must number at most this many.

name.description: Unique identifier for the rule. Use kebab-case.

description.description: Human-readable explanation, shown in validation output.

severity.description: error = validation fails; warning = advisory, does not fail validation.

match.description: Selects which work items this rule applies to. If omitted, the rule applies to all items. When multiple fields are specified, all conditions must be true (AND).

require.description: Assertions that must hold for each matching item. When multiple fields are specified, all assertions must be true (AND).

count.description: Limits how many items in the entire project may match. Used for collection-wide rules like WIP limits.

min.description: At least this many items must match.

max.description: At most this many items may match.
